_provenance: Digitized 2026-07-21 from OSM pavilion footprint (way 1112264619) + Esri World Imagery + USGS 3DEP 1 m DEM (MI_CharlevoixCounty_2018_A18). All anchor values INFERRED (imagery/OSM-derived), not surveyed. Bowl rake/rise/extent are MEASURED from the DEM.
stage_rect_note: OSM footprint is a CIRCLE (22 nodes, all radii 7.0-8.3 m; mean 7.75 m -> 15.5 m / 51 ft diameter). Unlike the Meijer lens-canopy there is no long axis, so the audience azimuth CANNOT be derived from the footprint orientation — it was taken from the DEM seating fall line instead.
stage_long_axis_note: circular roof — no long axis exists; field kept null rather than fabricated
audience_facing_basis: MEASURED from DEM: the only monotonic rising sector around the pavilion is az 275-345 (seating side), centred az 310; audience therefore faces the reciprocal, az 130. Cross-checked against Esri imagery, which shows ~5-6 concentric turf terrace bands in exactly that sector. +/-10 deg.
arc_center_note: stage-front center = pavilion centroid moved 7.75 m (the circular footprint radius) along az 310, i.e. the shell edge facing the seating

breaks_ft._basis: measured 2026-07-21 from the DEM centerline profile along az 310: flat apron 586.6-586.7 ft holds to s=8-10 ft (row 1 / front of seating), continuous rise to 597.6-598.0 ft at s=66-74 ft, then a flat promenade plateau (597.4-598.1 ft) all the way out to s=164 ft. Top of seating and rim crest coincide — there is no separate rim berm.

fan_deg._basis: inferred from the DEM radial sweep (sector with short, steep, monotonic rise) cross-checked against the imagery terrace bands; beyond az 345 and below az 275 the ground still rises but as long shallow topography toward Bridge St (rake ~6-10%), which is street grade, not seating. +/-10 deg.

stage_frontage_ft._basis: INFERRED and weakly constrained: the pavilion roof fully occludes the performance deck in every available overhead image, so no deck edge can be digitized. Bounded ABOVE by the OSM roof circle (51 ft diameter, independent contributor digitization) and BELOW by assuming a ~5 ft roof overhang each side. NO published stage dimension exists for this venue (see SOURCES.md failed-search log) — there is no independent cross-check, and this number must not be treated as measured.

stage_depth_ft._basis: INFERRED: circular structure, so depth and frontage share the same bound (the roof diameter, 51 ft). Same occlusion limit and same absence of any published spec as stage_frontage_ft; cross-check is the OSM circle only.

roof.value: permanent roof over the STAGE ONLY; audience is open-air on the hillside terraces
roof._note: circular roof reads clearly in imagery and as a ~6-7 ft mound in the bare-earth DEM; no audience cover anywhere in the seating sector

stage_lighting.value: UNRESOLVED — permanently mounted light fixtures on metal arrays are present on the roof underside, but whether they are THEATRICAL or ARCHITECTURAL lighting is not established
stage_lighting._basis: OBSERVED by the project owner from a passing vehicle, ~July 2026, undated and unmeasured. Establishes that a permanent ceiling-mounted fixture array EXISTS; does NOT establish its type, count, circuiting, control position, dimming, or who may use it. VERIFY by site visit or by calling Charlevoix Rec Dept 231-547-3253.
stage_lighting._counter_evidence: IMPORTANT (added 2026-07-21, second search pass): the APA Great Public Spaces write-up states 'Both the Harbormaster's Building and Odmark performing arts pavilion are heated and cooled with geothermal energy; MOTION SENSORS CONTROL BUILDING LIGHTING' (https://www.planning.org/greatplaces/spaces/2009/eastpark.htm). Motion-sensor control describes an automatic ARCHITECTURAL/service lighting system — a theatrical rig would never be switched that way. This is a genuine counter-indication to reading the observed array as stage lighting. Three readings remain open: (a) theatrical rig, (b) motion-sensored building lighting mounted on a grid, (c) both present.
stage_lighting._conflict: The City's Performance Pavilion Use Policy (DocumentCenter/View/408, rev. 2025-11-20) states 'Users must provide their own equipment.' Combined with the motion-sensor evidence, the balance of PUBLISHED evidence now leans toward the pavilion providing building/service lighting rather than a performance lighting rig. Treat as UNRESOLVED and do NOT credit this venue with stage lighting in any comparison.

building_services.value: heated and cooled with geothermal energy; motion sensors control building lighting
building_services._basis: APA Great Public Spaces 2009 write-up (https://www.planning.org/greatplaces/spaces/2009/eastpark.htm), verbatim. Establishes that the pavilion is a serviced, conditioned BUILDING — not an open shelter — which implies substantial electrical service exists even though its capacity is undocumented. Consistent with the keyed lockable room in the use policy.

construction_cost.value: $1,490,642 for the pavilion/performance building; $2,610,597 East Park construction; $4,829,500 marina construction
construction_cost._basis: City of Charlevoix DDA accomplishments ledger, listed under 2005 (https://www.charlevoixmi.gov/315/Accomplishments), verbatim: 'Removal of Existing Band Shell, Fish Pond, and Restroom Building - Construction of a new pavilion/performance building, with amphitheater seating ($1,490,642)'. NOTE the phrase 'with amphitheater seating' — the hillside terraces are DESIGNED, CONSTRUCTED seating, not incidental lawn. Verified directly 2026-07-21.

design_team.value: pavilion architect Mark Buday Architect PLLC (Harbor Springs MI); acoustical consultant Wallmark Consulting (Traverse City); civil/structural Performance Engineers Inc. (Charlevoix); mechanical/electrical Peter Basso and Associates (Troy); marina designer United Design Associates (Cheboygan)
design_team._basis: Read from the 2005-07 'East Park & Marina Improvements' project plan sheet during the 2026-07-21 capacity search. PARTIALLY CORROBORATED ONLY: Mark Buday is confirmed independently as a principal architect in Harbor Springs MI (now Buday+Kruzel Architects), but no independent source ties the firm to this building. Treat the roster as plan-sheet-sourced, not independently confirmed. Relevant because an ACOUSTICAL CONSULTANT was engaged, supporting 'natural acoustics' as a designed property rather than marketing copy.

funding.value: Michigan State Waterways Commission grant assistance (marina); no Michigan Natural Resources Trust Fund grant tied to the pavilion
funding._basis: 2005-07 project plan sheet credit block; cross-checked against the 'Previous DNR Grants' table in the city's Recreation Plan Chapter 4 (charlevoixmi.gov/DocumentCenter/View/131), which lists no MNRTF grant for this project.

sound_system._basis: City Use Policy: 'Users must provide their own equipment.' The venue is described in city/visitor copy as a 'stone band shell with natural acoustics', which implies acoustic reinforcement by shell geometry rather than an installed PA. No sound-system spec found in any source.

electrical_service._basis: no amperage, circuit count, outlet, or shore-power detail published in the facility page, Use Request Form, or Use Policy. A permanent lighting rig implies service exists, but its capacity is undocumented.

enclosed_room.value: the pavilion contains a keyed, lockable room
enclosed_room._basis: Use Policy requires users to 'return all keys to the Recreation Department located in City Hall' and refers to 'this room'; consistent with a control/storage space in the shell, but its size and function are not stated.

operating_limits.value: use limited to 2 hours, must end by 9:30 p.m.; max 2 uses per calendar month, not sequential days; fee $120 resident / $200 non-resident; no admission charge without City Clerk business licensing
operating_limits._basis: City of Charlevoix Performance Pavilion Use Policy, rev. 2025-11-20 (DocumentCenter/View/408) + Use Request Form (DocumentCenter/View/407)

noise_standard.value: discretionary, no dB limit — Rec Dept weighs whether an event is 'likely to generate noise that would adversely affect people who are not attending the event'

row_count_note: turf/stone terrace bands on the hillside, no fixed seats found in any source

row_count_inferred.value: ~5-6 terrace bands between r=12 m and r=25 m, plus open lawn and a flat promenade above; no fixed seating
row_count_inferred._basis: inferred: counted as concentric light/dark turf bands in Esri World Imagery in the az 275-345 sector; the 1 m DEM smooths them into a continuous ramp and CANNOT resolve individual risers

ada_circulation_note: level paved plaza wraps the pavilion at the 586.6 ft apron; a paved promenade runs along the 597.6-598.0 ft rim above the seating; imagery shows paved walks flanking the terraces on both sides connecting the two levels. Whether any of these meets running-slope/cross-slope code is UNVERIFIED — no as-built or civil drawing was found.
backdrop_note: audience faces ~SE (az 130) across the pavilion; Round Lake and the municipal marina lie immediately beyond and to the left of the stage (flat water return at 579.4 ft NAVD88 begins ~25-30 m out on az 30-140), so the water reads as the stage backdrop from the seating. UNLIKE Petoskey, the band shell is a closed stone structure — it occludes the water directly behind the stage; the lake is seen past the shell's flanks, not through the stage opening.
capacity_basis: UNKNOWN — CONFIRMED ABSENT after two independent search passes (2026-07-21). No design capacity, fire-code occupant load, press crowd estimate, or venue marketing claim exists in any publicly indexed source. The second pass specifically checked the document classes most likely to carry one: the City's DNR-required Parks & Recreation Master Plan facility inventory (two editions, full PDF text — East Park lists amenities only), Michigan Natural Resources Trust Fund and State Waterways Commission grant records, Michigan Municipal League placemaking material, DDA/council accomplishment ledgers, county building-safety occupancy documents, regional press coverage of Venetian Festival and Live on the Lake, the design firm, and Wikidata Q34871967. The city's Use Request Form asks the applicant to state an 'expected number of attendees' and sets no cap. Deliberately left null rather than estimated from bowl area — see SOURCES.md failed-search log.

dem_verification.terrace_preservation: PARTIALLY VERIFIED — the DEM cleanly resolves the overall bowl (continuous 11.3 ft rise, hillshade fall line matches the imagery terrace sector), but it does NOT resolve the individual terrace risers that imagery shows; terrace COUNT therefore stays inferred_imagery while rake/rise stay measured_dem.
dem_verification.known_voids: (1) Round Lake is a flattened water return at 579.4 ft NAVD88. (2) CRITICAL: the pavilion structure is NOT removed from this 'bare-earth' surface — it reads as a ~6-7 ft mound (593.3 ft at the roof apex vs a 586.6 ft surrounding apron), so NO ground elevation, stage-deck height, or deck geometry under the shell may be taken from this DEM. This is the same covered-structure limitation that disqualified the Vail comparator, but here it affects only the stage footprint, not the seating.

route_concept.value: level paved apron plaza at stage level (586.6 ft) and a level paved promenade at the rim (597.6-598.0 ft), linked by paved walks flanking the terraces — both ends of the bowl reachable on paved park circulation without a dedicated ramp structure

vertical_drop_ft.value: 11.3 ft measured rise from row 1 (586.7 ft) to top of seating (598.0 ft) on the az 310 centerline

dispersion.value: plausibly the stage-level apron and the rim promenade (two elevations); no accessible-seating locations are designated in any public source found

redundancy.value: paved walks appear on both flanks of the terrace sector

sightline_preservation.value: unverified; lawn/terrace venue with no fixed seats and no published accessible-seating plan
